channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Tech giant OpenAI files for US initial public offering
item.description: OpenAI did not disclose the size or terms of the offering and said a timeline has not yet been determined.

item.title: UN human rights leader calls for Cuba sanctions to be ‘lifted immediately’
item.description: Volker Turk, the UN's high commissioner for human rights, warns that 'children are dying' as result of US oil blockade.

item.title: US confirms it denied entry to Somali referee set to take part in World Cup
item.description: Award-winning referee Omar Artan was 'determined to be inadmissible due to vetting concerns' after arriving in Miami.

item.title: Pope Leo makes visit to Spain’s Almudena Cathedral
item.description: Crowds in Madrid were delighted to see Pope Leo make a visit to Santa Maria de la Almudena Cathedral with Queen Sofia.

item.title: EU planning to add to $1.5 trillion sanctions hit on Russia
item.description: New proposed listings target Russia's 'military industrial complex, human rights violators and propagandists'.

item.title: Italian prosecutors to probe Israeli minister Ben-Gvir over flotilla abuse
item.description: Rome is the second in Europe to launch a probe into Israel's treatment of activists detained as they headed to Gaza.

item.title: Could Iran and the US play each other at the World Cup?
item.description: Could Iran and the US play each other at the World Cup?

item.title: ‘We basically lost’: Tel Aviv residents react after Israel-Iran strikes
item.description: Residents in Tel Aviv voiced mixed reactions after a day of missile exchanges between Israel and Iran.

item.title: Gaza cancer patient waits for a chance at life
item.description: A school director and novelist in Gaza needs urgent life-saving cancer surgery that she cannot access inside the Strip.

item.title: How significant is Xi Jinping’s visit to North Korea?
item.description: Beijing is Pyongyang's main political and economic backer.

item.title: France and Germany scrap fighter jet in hit to European defence cooperation
item.description: The termination of the plan comes as Washington increases pressure on Europe to become more militarily independent.

item.title: Will the FIFA World Cup be the economic bonanza US cities were promised?
item.description: Cities remain optimistic despite lagging hotel bookings and high ticket prices that might deter foreign travellers.

item.title: CCTV captures moment of deadly strike on Zaporizhzhia, Ukraine
item.description: CCTV footage captured the moment a missile struck Zaporizhzhia, Ukraine, killing two people and injuring 23.

item.title: US congressman demands probe into Israel’s 1967 attack on USS Liberty
item.description: Thomas Massie suggests the deadly assault was not an accident and calls on the US government to honour its survivors.

item.title: Floods tear through homes and farmland in China’s Guizhou province
item.description: Drone footage shows entire neighbourhoods, roads and farmland submerged by floodwaters in China's southwestern Guizhou.

item.title: China’s Xi Jinping and North Korea’s Kim Jong Un meet in Pyongyang
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping and North Korean leader Kim Jong Un met on Monday at a rare summit in Pyongyang.

item.title: Albanian PM Rama vows to push on with Kushner-linked luxury resort
item.description: Albanian Prime Minister Rama vowed to press ahead with a billion-dollar luxury resort project linked to Jared Kushner.

item.title: Southern Philippines hit by 7.8-magnitude earthquake
item.description: Offshore quake rocks Mindanao, killing at least 35 and injuring more than 200.

item.title: NATO jets shoot down drone over Latvia, extending Ukraine spillover fears
item.description: The drone entered Latvian airspace due to 'Russian electronic warfare', the military says.

item.title: US imposes restrictions on 100 Nicaraguan officials after activist’s death
item.description: The death of Indigenous leader Brooklyn Rivera has prompted calls from UN experts for an independent investigation.

item.title: Moment Israeli helicopter fires missile on Gaza City target
item.description: Footage shows the moment an Israeli helicopter launched a missile at an unknown target in the Gaza City neighbourhood.

item.title: WHO chief lauds Uganda’s response to Ebola outbreak
item.description: Tedros said the country had responded promptly and capably, but called for it to reopen the border with DR Congo.

item.title: Airline profits plummet as US fuel costs nearly double
item.description: IATA forecasts $350bn airline fuel bill for 2026, with profits dipping to their weakest margin since the COVID-19 years.

item.title: UN watchdog, Western nations call on Iran to restart nuclear cooperation
item.description: IAEA chief Rafael Grossi urges Tehran to re-engage. US and E3 demand information on whereabouts of uranium.

item.title: FIFA World Cup: the politics and profits of the beautiful game
item.description: The World Cup has yet to start, but politics, war, and controversy are already competing with football.
